title: Bulk report - Week 14
summary:
Capesize
The Capesize market started the week slowly due to the Hari Raya Puasa holiday in Singapore but gained momentum as trading resumed. The Pacific saw a midweek rebound, driven by strong C5 activity, with all three miners fixing in the low $9s. A tightening tonnage list and solid cargo volumes supported rates, while increased coal demand pushed timecharter earnings higher.

children.title: Capesize
children.summary: The Capesize market started the week slowly due to the Hari Raya Puasa holiday in Singapore but gained momentum as trading resumed. The Pacific saw a midweek rebound, driven by strong C5 activity, with all three miners fixing in the low $9s.

children.title: Panamax
children.summary: A compelling week for the Panamax sector, the uncertainty caused by the US tariff measure seeping into the market as momentum stalled as the week ended. All week, the Atlantic provided a positional divide with the North Atlantic tonnage under pressure owing to a lack of demand.

children.title: Ultramax/Supramax
children.summary: In what has been a remarkable week for international trade, the sector was anything but remarkable. The Atlantic had a rather slow week, perhaps with an air of caution.

children.title: Handysize
children.summary: The market this week saw minimal visible activity across both basins, with the overall sentiment remaining flat. The Continent and Mediterranean markets stayed subdued, with very little new information emerging, and rates generally held steady.
